QQQ.data_anomaly: QQQ price $714.71 is outside expected range ($250-$700). Verify data source — may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: Wednesday's session saw the S&P 500 add 0.77% to 742.31 while the Nasdaq surged 1.39% to 714.71, buoyed by a 3.39% jump in SOXX as ASML lifted its 2026 sales outlook. April PPI data released Wednesday came in hot, stoking inflation fears with energy prices responsible for 40% of the increase, and odds of a 2026 rate hike rose to nearly 30% by December (per Yahoo Finance). The Hormuz crisis lingers at day 23, keeping WTI at $100.95 and pressuring rate-sensitive names.
commodities_note: WTI slipped 0.39% to $100.95 but remains above $100 for the third straight session. Gold edged up 0.53% to $4,705.70, and the US10Y yield ticked higher to 4.48%, extending its rise and reinforcing the hawkish inflation narrative. DXY was flat at 98.45. QQQ data shows a potential anomaly at $714.71, outside its expected range—verify source.

accumulate_gates.note: All mechanical gates PASS, but momentum_warn is True (weak recent tape). ACCUMULATE requires either an explicit multi-year structural support cite (200-day SMA / prior cycle low / named floor) OR an imminent hard catalyst within 7 days (specific date). Generic fundamental praise does NOT clear the bar — default to WATCH if neither anchor applies.

writeup.headline: WRD Q1 revenue +58%, R:R 2.7:1 — ADR risk keeps entry cautious.
writeup.what_to_do: WeRide’s Q1 revenue jumped 58% YoY (per MarketBeat, May 13), improving R:R to 2.7:1 with a tight invalidation at $7.40. Fragility gate: ADR/China delisting risk and single-thesis driver cap signal to WATCH. Upgrades to ACCUMULATE on a sustained reclaim above $8.00 with volume and a non-China contract win. PAPER TRADE — under evaluation.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks remain CAUTION with overbought RSIs and persistent NIM compression. DBS and OCBC are extended +6.9% above their 50-day lines, while UOB’s RSI 68 blocks entry. The dual rate headwind from US10Y at 4.48% and MAS S$NEER appreciation keeps the cluster in a defensive posture — no relief is likely before the next MAS decision in October 2026.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired by a tight monetary policy backdrop, with no near-term NIM catalyst in sight.
singapore.key_development: US10Y climbed to 4.48%, further above the 4.20% threshold where SGD funding costs begin to ease; day 55 of no relief.

semiconductors.summary: The semiconductor cluster saw a broad relief rally with SOXX surging 3.4% to 528.29, led by ASML’s raised 2026 outlook and TSEM’s $1.3B photonics deals. However, every name remains CAUTION, with extreme extension the dominant theme: AMD, MU, NVDA, INTC, LITE, TSEM, 000660.KS, and NOK all sit more than 20% above their 50-day lines, making entry impossible. The bifurcation is between stocks with confirmed revenue catalysts (ASML, TSM, TSEM) and those riding pure momentum (AMD, MU, INTC, NOK) — the former have a slightly more defensible pullback case.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish but tactically extremely extended; a broad correction or earnings-driven reset is needed before any fresh capital deployment.
semiconductors.key_development: SOXX rebounded 3.4% as ASML lifted 2026 guidance, but 8 of 11 members are still in an extreme >20% extension block.

big_tech.summary: BigTech remains mixed with only MSFT holding near its 50-day and PLTR sinking below it. Amazon and Alphabet are both extreme extensions, while MSFT’s near-entry setup offers the lone actionable watch within the cluster. The broader inflation and rate sensitivity continue to weigh on mega-cap multiples, with MSFT’s 20.9x forward PE standing out as a relative value play among peers.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally intact but tactically stretched except for MSFT; rate-hike fears cap near-term upside for the group.
big_tech.key_development: April PPI came in hot, reinforcing the hawkish narrative and keeping upside pressure on yields — MSFT’s 50-day at $398.76 is the key level to watch.

interconnected.reason: Receiving the same $2B NVDA photonics investment as LITE; moves in tandem on AI optics demand signals.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 10-15% from current levels and NVDA’s May 20 earnings reaffirm optics spending.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Primary beneficiary of NVDA Spectrum-X back-end networking and hyperscaler AI capex — direct linkage to NVDA, MSFT, and AMZN spending.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if ANET retraces 8-10% from its recent highs on a broader tech selloff, with the next catalyst being NVDA’s May 20 earnings.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom AI ASIC and optical interconnect supplier; revenue directly tied to AMZN Trainium, GOOG TPU, and general AI infrastructure buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if MRVL pulls back 12-15% and next earnings (tentatively late May) confirm data-center revenue acceleration.

active_risks: Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed since Feb 28; WTI persists above $100, feeding inflation and suppressing rate-sensitive equities.
US-China trade war escalates with Trump threatening new 100% tariffs and tech export limits; supply chain uncertainty for semis intensifies.
Kevin Warsh’s expected confirmation as Fed chair Wednesday adds near-term policy uncertainty; hawks may gain influence amid hot PPI data.
Samsung potential labor strike from May 21 threatens HBM supply and amplifies memory price volatility.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil above $100 is directly lifting energy stocks and keeping a bid under defense names like PLTR, but is compressing tech multiples via the rate channel. SOXX’s 3.4% bounce on AI demand is at odds with the broader hawkish macro; a sudden rate shock could unwind the rally. Singapore banks remain the most exposed to the oil-inflation dynamic, with MAS S$NEER strength adding a second layer of margin pressure.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: April PPI report released, coming in hotter than expected and raising rate-hike odds to 30% by December (per Yahoo Finance). ASML lifted its 2026 sales outlook, buoying semis. Kevin Warsh confirmation vote is scheduled for Wednesday.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold off on new entries; the combination of rising yields, extreme semis extensions, and Singapore bank compression leaves no clean entry. Wait for a pullback in tech and a break of US10Y below 4.20% before deploying capital.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — extreme extensions and tight monetary conditions block almost all entries
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Nineteen of 21 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, with only MSFT (near 50-day) and WRD (watch) providing potential entry setups. Hot PPI and rising yields reinforce the defensive posture.
